From the Provisions of the Lokstedt 8 Development Plan for Exceeding the Southern Building Boundary by 1 m through the Dormer on a Width of 4.97 m:
Justification: The exemption is granted.
Granted Building Regulation Deviations:
For Waiving the Requirement for a Continuous Necessary Staircase to the Top Attic Floor (§ 32 Abs. 1 HBauO):
Condition: Suitable signage for the escape route in the attic (staircase offset) in both directions by signs according to BGV A8/DIN 4844.
For Waiving the Requirement for a Necessary Staircase for Apartment 4 (§ 33 Abs. 1 HBauO in conjunction with § 29 Abs. 4 HBauO):
Condition:
The building must be occupied by only one family.
The basement must be separated with fire-resistant walls and a connecting door as a fire-retardant, smoke-tight, and self-closing closure (T30-RS door).
The internal staircase must be constructed as a necessary staircase according to § 32 Abs. 4 HBauO.
For Waiving the Requirement for a Necessary Staircase for Apartments 8, 9, and 10 (§ 33 Abs. 1 HBauO in conjunction with § 29 Abs. 4 HBauO):
Condition:
The main staircase as the 1st escape route must also be constructed as a necessary staircase in the top floor according to § 33 HBauO. The staircase DIN 18065 must also be observed for this upper part.
The internal staircases must be constructed as necessary staircases according to § 32 Abs. 4 HBauO.
Additional Conditions:
Construction must not begin until the following inspection items have been approved in a supplementary notice:
Structural stability: The required proof according to § 14 of the BauVorlagenVerordnung (BauVorlVO) must be submitted for inspection.
Additional Information:
Notice: The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions and does not authorize the commencement of work.

Granted Exemptions:
Granted Exemptions:
Exception under § 4 of the Tree Protection Ordinance and § 39 Abs. 5 BNatSchG:
Allowed Actions:
Felling of the construction-impeding maple (Tree Nr. 1) between October 1 and February 28.
Carrying out the requested works in the protected crown and root area of the beech (Tree Nr. 2) under the supervision of a certified tree expert.
Tree Preservation:
Other protected trees must be preserved and protected according to DIN 18920 and environmental regulations throughout the construction process.
Additional Conditions:
Replacement Planting:
The felling permission is expressly conditioned on fulfilling the replacement planting.
Replacement planting must be maintained permanently; in case of loss, equivalent replacements must be planted.
Special Tree Protection:
Compliance with tree protection guidelines and supervision by a certified tree expert must be proven to the designated authority before starting work.
Compensation Payment:
A compensation amount of €5,000 must be paid for nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures.
The compensation for the felled maple is calculated based on the required value calculation model, resulting in a need for 7 replacement trees or €7,000. Two replacement trees are to be planted on-site, and the remaining value is settled with a €5,000 compensation payment.

A qualified gardening company must carry out planting and associated measures, with supervision by a landscape architect.
